cards.back: A decision tree is a tree-like model for making decisions, used in computer science to solve problems by recursively partitioning the problem space

cards.front: What is P in the context of computational complexity?
cards.back: P refers to the class of decision problems that can be solved in polynomial time by a deterministic Turing machine

cards.front: What is NP in the context of computational complexity?
cards.back: NP refers to the class of decision problems where a solution can be verified in polynomial time, but may not always be found in polynomial time

NP-Complete problems are a subclass of NP problems that are at least as hard as the hardest problems in NP, meaning that if a problem can be solved in polynomial time, then all NP problems can be solved in polynomial time
An NP-Complete problem is a decision problem that is both NP and NP-Complete, meaning that it can be verified in polynomial time, but is at least as hard as the hardest problems in NP
